summary: Comprehensive backend testing of WapiHub messaging API. 29/29 pytest cases passed (100%) across health, auth (register/login/logout/me/refresh cookies), admin (stats + customer CRUD + key regen + RBAC), customer profile (/me), WhatsApp sessions lifecycle (create/status/list/delete + ownership 404s), and public X-API-Key API (auth + no-connected-session). Discovered & fixed a critical Baileys v7 import bug in /app/wa-service/server.js that made every session-start return 500. After the fix, FastAPI -> Node Baileys integration works end-to-end (QR flow not tested as out-of-scope per request).

critical.issue: Baileys v7 has NO default export — server.js used `import baileysPkg from '@whiskeysockets/baileys'` and destructured `default: makeWASocket, useMultiFileAuthState, ...` which are all undefined. Caused 500 with `useMultiFileAuthState is not a function` on every session create. Fixed by switching to namespace import (`import * as baileysPkg`) and dropping `default:` from destructure.

action_items: Verify the wa-service Baileys import fix in /app/wa-service/server.js (lines 9-17) is acceptable and committed.
Manually scan a real WhatsApp QR via the dashboard end-to-end to validate the actual send flow (out-of-scope for automated tests).

Consider returning a clearer 4xx (e.g. 400/409 with a 'session not connected' detail) from POST /api/messages/send when Baileys send fails, instead of returning 200 with status='failed' + error string. Current behavior is acceptable but mixes successful HTTP semantics with failed business outcome.
Add a /api/auth/refresh test once a real refresh-cookie flow is exercised by the frontend (endpoint exists and works manually).

wa_supervisor.start() is invoked synchronously inside FastAPI's startup event with a 0.5s sleep; on slow boots the first /api/health request may race the Node process. Health currently passes but consider a readiness probe on Node /health before returning.
critical_code_review_comments: wa-service/server.js: Baileys v7.x dropped its CommonJS default export (the module's `default` is `{}`). The previous code path was always broken — this means session creation has never worked since deploy. Add a minimal Node smoke test (e.g. `node -e "import('@whiskeysockets/baileys').then(m => console.log(typeof m.makeWASocket))"`) to CI.
server.py admin seeding (lines 600-624): seed_admin correctly upserts the admin password if it changed in env — playbook compliant. Good.
auth.py: bcrypt hashing uses gensalt() (yields $2b$ format) — verified compliant. JWT uses HS256 with env secret, httpOnly+secure+samesite=none cookies — correct for cross-site preview URL.
server.py /admin/customers PATCH: silently no-ops if no fields are provided (returns the existing user). Acceptable but could return 400 for empty patch.
server.py /messages/send: returns 200 even on failed send (status='failed'). Frontend must inspect body, not status code. Consider 502 if Baileys returns error.
server.py /sessions DELETE: still returns 200 if WA logout fails (silently swallowed). OK pragmatically but log the exception.
No rate-limiting / brute-force lockout on /auth/login (playbook recommends lockout after 5 fails). Currently any number of bad attempts allowed. Consider adding fastapi-limiter or in-app counter.

CORS: allow_origins defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True — browsers will reject this combination. In practice REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is same-origin via ingress so cookies work; but if CORS_ORIGINS env is unset the configuration is invalid. Make CORS_ORIGINS required.

wa_supervisor.py uses subprocess.Popen with preexec_fn=os.setsid; if the FastAPI worker dies abnormally the Node child may be reparented to PID 1 and continue running but new backend boots will spawn another node — port 3001 collision possible on hot-reload. Consider checking port-in-use before spawn or using supervisord directly for the Node service.
updated_files: /app/wa-service/server.js (fixed Baileys v7 import — namespace import instead of default)
test_credentials: admin@wapihub.com / admin123 (seeded). Customer accounts created on the fly per test run with TEST_*@example.com prefix and cleaned up via admin DELETE.
seed_data_creation: Each test run registers ephemeral TEST_customer_*@example.com customer + creates short-lived sessions; created admin-side customers are tracked and deleted in a session-scoped teardown fixture (created_customer_ids).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green after wa-service Baileys import fix. WA Node service is auto-spawned by FastAPI on port 3001 — restart backend (sudo supervisorctl restart backend) after any change to wa-service/server.js. /api/health will report wa_service:'down' if Node isn't up. To re-run: `python -m pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. EmailStr in pydantic rejects .test/.local TLDs — use @example.com for synthetic test emails. Real WhatsApp QR scanning is intentionally NOT tested.
rca_of_the_issue: Bug: POST /api/sessions returned 500 because wa-service called useMultiFileAuthState as a function but it was undefined. Reproduction: curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3001/sessions/x/start -> {error:'useMultiFileAuthState is not a function'}. Root cause: @whiskeysockets/baileys v7.0.0-rc.9 ships ESM with named exports only — its `default` export is an empty object, so destructuring `{ default: makeWASocket, useMultiFileAuthState, ... }` from `import baileysPkg from '@whiskeysockets/baileys'` yielded all undefined. Fix: changed to `import * as baileysPkg from '@whiskeysockets/baileys'` and removed the `default:` alias. Verified with `node -e "import('@whiskeysockets/baileys').then(m => console.log(typeof m.makeWASocket, typeof m.useMultiFileAuthState))"` -> both 'function'. Mitigation: also add a startup self-check in wa-service that throws if these symbols are not functions, so the failure is caught at boot rather than first user request.
